description.p: [0001] The present invention relates to photocatalytic systems for the reduction of nitrogen oxides (NOx) in air. NOx emissions from combustion processes contribute to acid rain and tropospheric ozone formation.
[0002] Conventional photocatalysts based on TiO2 suffer from limited visible-light absorption, restricting their efficiency under typical outdoor illumination.
[0003] The disclosed system employs a Z-scheme heterojunction between a visible-light-absorbing semiconductor and a co-catalyst, enabling spatial separation of photogenerated electron-hole pairs.
[0005] In a preferred embodiment, the co-catalyst comprises noble-metal nanoparticles dispersed on the support surface at a loading of 0.5–2.0 wt%.
